title: cuphea urens
intro: Cuphea urens, also known as the firecracker plant, is a type of flowering shrub native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The leaves and flowers of Cuphea urens have been used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory problems, while also providing antioxidant and antiseptic benefits. Herbal preparations of Cuphea urens, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
